title: First-Time Home Buyer Mortgages in Greater Vancouver
metaTitle: First-Time Home Buyer Mortgage — Greater Vancouver, BC
metaDescription: First-time home buyer mortgages, programs, and incentives across Greater Vancouver and BC. PTT exemption, Tax-Free First Home Savings Account, RRSP HBP and 30-year amortization explained.
intro: Buying your first home in Greater Vancouver is the single largest financial decision most people make. The right mortgage strategy can save you tens of thousands — and there are several first-time buyer programs that compound to make a real difference.

body.heading: Programs that actually move the needle
paragraphs: There are five federal and provincial first-time buyer programs worth knowing about, and three of them are genuinely useful. The First Home Savings Account (FHSA) lets you contribute up to $8,000 per year, $40,000 total, with both the deposit and the withdrawal tax-free when used for a qualifying first home — it's the most powerful program of the bunch. The RRSP Home Buyers' Plan (HBP) lets you withdraw up to $60,000 per person ($120,000 for a couple) from your RRSP toward a down payment, repaid over 15 years interest-free.

The BC Property Transfer Tax first-time buyer exemption gives full PTT relief up to $500,000 of fair market value and partial relief up to $835,000 — typically $8,000–$16,000 in tax savings on a Greater Vancouver purchase. The federal 30-year amortization for first-time buyers (announced in 2024 and now active) lowers your stress-tested payment by roughly 8–12%, which directly increases what you can qualify for.

body.heading: How much down payment do you really need?
paragraphs: The minimum down payment in Canada is 5% on the first $500,000 of purchase price and 10% on the portion from $500,000 to $1,500,000. On a $950,000 Burnaby townhouse, that's $25,000 + $45,000 = $70,000 minimum. Above $1.5M, you need 20% down on the entire purchase price.

Below 20% down, your mortgage must be insured (CMHC, Sagen or Canada Guaranty) and a one-time premium of 2.4–4.0% gets added to your mortgage balance. That premium is generally rolled into your payments so you don't write a separate cheque, but it does mean you owe slightly more than the purchase price minus your down payment.
bullets: West Vancouver detached at $2,200,000 → $440,000 minimum down (uninsured)
Add ~2–4% closing costs (PTT, legal, adjustments) on top of your down payment

body.heading: What lenders actually look at
paragraphs: Pre-approval comes down to four numbers: gross household income, total monthly debt obligations, credit score, and confirmed down payment. The federal stress test applies: you'll be qualified at the higher of your contract rate plus 2% or the 5.25% federal floor, whichever is higher.

Credit-wise, you want a beacon score of 680+ for the most competitive insured rates, though I've placed first-time buyer files with scores in the 620s. Below 600, you're looking at credit unions or B-lenders — still doable, but pricier.

whoItsFor: Have never owned a principal residence anywhere in the world
Are a Canadian citizen or permanent resident living in BC
Plan to occupy the home as your principal residence within 92 days
Have at least 5% saved (or in an FHSA / RRSP HBP)
requirements: Two recent paystubs (or 2 years of self-employed financials)
T4s or NOAs for the last 2 years
90-day history of down payment in your account
Proof of any FHSA / RRSP HBP balances you'll use

process.detail: 20-minute call to find your realistic price range and the right program mix for your situation.
I underwrite your file fully and lock in a rate hold for 90–120 days.
House-hunt with confidence; we move to subject-removal once you find the right property.

faqs.question: Can I combine the FHSA and RRSP Home Buyers' Plan?
faqs.answer: Yes — and you should. Maxing both for a couple gives you up to $80,000 from FHSAs and $120,000 from RRSPs, all tax-free or tax-deferred. That's $200,000 of down payment for two people who've been saving for a few years.

faqs.question: What's the cheapest neighbourhood to buy in Greater Vancouver as a first-time buyer?
faqs.answer: In 2026, condo entry-points are typically Surrey Central, Coquitlam Town Centre, Maple Ridge and parts of Burnaby Heights. Townhouses run higher. I help clients align neighbourhood choice with what their numbers can actually support.

faqs.question: Is the BC Home Owner Mortgage and Equity Partnership still available?
faqs.answer: No — that program was discontinued in 2018. The current BC programs are the PTT exemption (active), and the federal FHSA, RRSP HBP and 30-year amortization options.

faqs.question: Can I qualify with student debt?
faqs.answer: Yes. Student loan minimum payments are included in your TDS calculation, but they don't disqualify you. Some lenders allow OSAP loans in repayment to be calculated at 1% of the balance instead of the actual payment, which helps.
